criteria_results.criterion: Tools page renders a React Flow canvas (not the old table view)
criteria_results.evidence: React Flow canvas rendered with application role, Controls (Zoom In/Out/Fit View), MiniMap, and Background. No table view present.

criteria_results.criterion: Toolbar has 'Add Tool' and 'Add Tool Section' buttons (top-left Panel)
criteria_results.evidence: Both 'Add Tool' and 'Add Tool Section' buttons visible in toolbar panel (Panel position=top-left per React Flow structure).

criteria_results.criterion: Zoom/pan controls (React Flow Controls + MiniMap + Background dots)
criteria_results.evidence: React Flow Controls (Zoom In, Zoom Out, Fit View buttons) and MiniMap both present in accessibility tree.

criteria_results.criterion: Summary sidebar when nothing selected: total monthly cost, annual cost, counts by status
criteria_results.evidence: Tools Summary panel visible with Monthly cost ($0/mo), Annual cost ($0/yr), and By Status breakdown showing Active: 0, Considering: 0, Cancelled: 0.

criteria_results.criterion: Empty state overlay when no tools exist
criteria_results.evidence: Empty state overlay shows 'No tools yet' with description 'Add tools to map your tech stack, or create groups to organize them' and CTA buttons.

criteria_results.criterion: Tool nodes display: name, status badge (active=green, considering=yellow, cancelled=gray), cost/mo
criteria_results.evidence: Cannot verify — 'Add Tool' POST /api/v1/tools returns 500: 'Could not find the position_x column of tools in the schema cache'. Migration 023 (adds position_x, position_y, status columns to tools table) has not been applied to the local Supabase instance.

criteria_results.criterion: Tool section nodes are resizable containers (like process section nodes, using NodeResizer)
criteria_results.evidence: Cannot verify — tool creation blocked by missing migration 023. Add Tool Section also likely blocked by missing tool_sections table.

criteria_results.criterion: Tools can be dragged — position persists via updateTool API (position_x, position_y)
criteria_results.evidence: Cannot verify — tool creation blocked by missing migration 023.

criteria_results.criterion: Tool sections can be dragged/resized — persists via updateToolSection API
criteria_results.evidence: Cannot verify — tool section creation blocked by missing migration 023 (tool_sections table not present).

criteria_results.criterion: Disabled 'Generate Summary' button has visible guidance text directing users to set maturity scores
criteria_results.evidence: Button 'Generate Summary' [disabled] present. Below it, paragraph with link 'Open the canvas to set maturity scores →' visible in accessibility tree (ref=e128/e129).

criteria_results.criterion: Guidance includes a navigable link to the first process canvas tab
criteria_results.evidence: Link href resolves to /w/c8f6619d-bdd8-4134-b7aa-3a70bcf599ea/fcfdce67-1453-46c9-a8f2-c3c0ef615619 — the workspace's first canvas tab. URL is valid and formed correctly.

criteria_results.criterion: When hasGapData is true, guidance text is hidden and button is fully functional
criteria_results.evidence: Accepted on build verification — guidance paragraph is conditionally rendered only when !hasGapData && firstProcessTab per BUILD_RESULT_2 notes. Cannot activate without existing maturity data in test DB.

criteria_results.criterion: Workspace list card shows creation date in YYYY-MM-DD format (consistent server/client)
criteria_results.evidence: Workspace card displays 'Created 2026-03-04' — YYYY-MM-DD format confirmed. Previously would show locale-formatted date (e.g. '3/4/2026').

criteria_results.criterion: No hydration mismatch warnings in browser console for workspace list
criteria_results.evidence: No hydration errors observed on /workspaces page. A Radix UI DropdownMenu ID mismatch exists on gap-analysis page but is pre-existing and unrelated to date formatting.

bugs_found.description: Migration 023 not applied to local Supabase — 'Add Tool' POST returns 500: 'Could not find the position_x column of tools in the schema cache'. Core tools canvas CRUD is completely non-functional. Canvas UI renders correctly but no tools or tool sections can be created.
bugs_found.steps_to_reproduce: 1. Navigate to /w/{workspaceId}/tools. 2. Click 'Add Tool' in toolbar. 3. POST /api/v1/tools returns 500.

bugs_found.description: Pre-existing: Radix UI DropdownMenuTrigger generates different auto-IDs on server vs client (id='radix-_R_9...' vs 'radix-_R_16...'). React hydration mismatch warning on every page with the user menu. Unrelated to current iteration.
bugs_found.steps_to_reproduce: 1. Navigate to any authenticated page. 2. Open browser console. 3. Observe 'A tree hydrated but some attributes of the server rendered HTML didn't match the client properties' error.

bugs_found.description: Pre-existing: GET /api/v1/improvement-ideas?workspace_id=... returns 500 on every workspace page load. Unrelated to current iteration.
bugs_found.steps_to_reproduce: 1. Navigate to any workspace page. 2. Check network tab — improvement-ideas request returns 500.

improvements_found.observation: The empty state CTA buttons say 'Add Tool' and 'Add Group' but the toolbar buttons say 'Add Tool' and 'Add Tool Section'. Inconsistent label ('Add Group' vs 'Add Tool Section') could confuse users about what they're creating.
improvements_found.suggested_approach: Align empty state button labels with toolbar labels — use 'Add Tool Section' instead of 'Add Group' in the empty state overlay.

improvements_found.observation: The guidance hint 'Open the canvas to set maturity scores →' is the only actionable text when no scores exist, but the existing 'Score steps to enable AI narrative.' text directly above the button doesn't direct users anywhere. Two separate hint messages could be consolidated.
improvements_found.design_principle: Nielsen H6: Recognition rather than recall
improvements_found.suggested_approach: Replace 'Score steps to enable AI narrative.' with the link text, or combine into one sentence: 'Open the canvas to set maturity scores on steps to enable AI narrative →'.
